calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil, via Zoom, for Michael Palmer, Administrator.

The court voices its concern with the unauthorized distributions made by the Administrator. Counsel reports of the difficult nature of this case especially working with his out-of-state client and that he was unaware of the unapproved distributions. The court points to the fact that there was no reference to the distribution issue in any of the preceding status reports filed with the court. Counsel states that his client had advised him that the money was being held in an estate account. The court and counsel discuss his request for extraordinary fees with counsel offering advocacy for his request.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-21-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Continued To Jun-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neill, via Zoom, for Michael Palmer, Administrator.

Counsel advises that his client had been out of the country until April and issues related to COVID have delayed the matter. The court points counsel to the discrepancy in assets reported to the court. Counsel reports that his client had used funds to care for his father. The court reminds counsel that those were unauthorized distributions. Counsel confirms and advises that the preceding will be addressed in his petition.

The matter is continued to Jun-21-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of An Ex Parte Petition To Increase Bond
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jmaes O'Neil for Michael Palmer.

Mr. O'Neil stated that he e-filed a petition and a waiver of bond yesterday. Status on filing of an ex parte petition to increase bond is continued to March 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Continued To Mar-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil for Michael Palmer.

Mr. O'Neil stated that they got a partial inventory filed for the CD and the checking account, that partial inventory #3 for the vanguard account was sent to the Probate Referee and that they got a final date of death statement that needs appraisal. The Court raised the issue of whether or not bond needs to be increased. Mr. O'Neil stated that the issue of bond came up because there was a creditor's claim in the amount of $45,000.00, that the Beneficiaries had originally waived bond, that the only Beneficiary was the Father of the Decedent who waived bond and is now deceased, and that there is no formal probate for an estate in Jersey for the Father. The Court stated that it does not think they have an effective waiver of bond, noted that letters were issued on June 14, 2016 and stated that this is way overdue. Status on filing of an ex parte petition to increase bond is set for February 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of a final inventory and appraisal is set for March 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for March 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Continued To Feb-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James O'Neil for Michael Palmer.

Mr. O'Neil stated that failure to appear at the last hearing was a clerical mis-hap and that they overlooked the hearing date on the order. Mr. O'Neil also stated that this a fairly simple estate, that there are six bank accounts for which they need to get statements from date of death, that his Client is out of State and that he is having a difficult time focusing his Client's attention on this. Status on close of estate is continued to February 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that it needs a final inventory and appraisal by January 29, 2020 and that in failing to do so, the Court will consider suspension. The order to appear is vacated.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: Vincent Yu for Michael Palmer (Administrator).

The Court stated that before it today is an Order to Appear for the Administrator and his Attorney. Mr. Yu apologized to the Court stating that the failure to appear at the last hearing was due to miscalendaring on their part, that there was a partial inventory done, that there were issues in obtaining date of death values for some of the assets, that one of their prior filings had incorrect information, that there are five or six accounts that require additional appraisal, and that the Administrator is currently on a leave doing research. The Court stated that a report on status of administration was filed, that a proposed order was not provided so that the Court could grant the continued administration of the estate, that the lack of a proposed order was part of the Order to Appear that was issued by the Court, that the proposed order was a subject of the 7/23/19 hearing, and that the Court is still in need of the proposed order as of today. Mr. Yu stated that a proposed order was dropped off a couple of times, that he is not sure what happened, and that he will submit a proposed order by mail. At the conclusion of Mr. Yu's comments, the Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration (subject to a written order) and took the Order to Appear off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vincent Yu for Michael Palmer (Administrator).

Mr. Yu stated that he has had some difficulty with his Client in New York, that another probate was opened in Massachusetts for the Decedent's Father, and that with the burden of that probate and his medical practice his Client has had little time to address this probate. Mr. Yu also stated that he believes his Client has provided all of the financial statements and that he needs time to put together a third final document. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to July 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
